# Numbers Chapter 16 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 167-169 | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_016_page_001.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0124-0125

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE REBELLION OF KORAH, DATHAN, AND ABIRAM:** Numbers 16 is the great rebellion narrative of the Torah — a triple-stranded uprising against the authority of Moses and Aaron that ends in unprecedented divine judgment. The chapter weaves together at least two (possibly three) rebel factions: Korah son of Izhar, a Levite who challenges the exclusive priesthood of Aaron (vv. 1-11, 16-24, 35); Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab of the tribe of Reuben, who challenge Moses's civil leadership and accuse him of failing to bring them into the promised land (vv. 12-15, 25-34); and the two hundred fifty chieftains who offer unauthorized incense (vv. 2, 17-18, 35). The verb that opens the chapter, וַיִּקַּח (va-yiqqakh, "and he took"), stands without a stated object — a grammatical anomaly that the ancient versions struggled with (LXX adds καὶ ἐλάλησεν, "and he spoke"). On the Leningrad Codex, this chapter begins on page 167 (folio 84 verso) and extends through page 169. The Vaticanus preserves the text across pages 124-125 (pa_0124-0125), where it appears as the end of chapter XIX and the beginning of chapter XX in the LXX numbering. The dual punishment — earth swallowing the household rebels, fire consuming the incense-offerers — marks this as the most devastating divine judgment since the golden calf (Exodus 32). The chapter forms the center of a chiastic structure in Numbers 11-20, where rebellion escalates toward its climax.

---

## The Rebellion Begins (vv. 1-3)

**1** And-took [va-yiqqakh] Korah [Qorakh] son of [ben] Izhar [Yitshar] son of [ben] Kohath [Qehat] son of [ben] Levi [Levi], and-Dathan [ve-Datan] and-Abiram [va-Aviram] sons of [benei] Eliab [Eli'av], and-On [ve-On] son of [ben] Peleth [Pelet], sons of [benei] Reuben [Re'uven];

> **Key terms:** וַיִּקַּח קֹרַח (va-yiqqakh Qorakh) = "and Korah took" — the qal vav-consecutive imperfect of לקח (laqakh, "to take"), used here without a direct object. This is one of the most debated grammatical anomalies in the Torah. Some take the verb absolutely ("took [himself aside]" or "took [men]"), while others emend or repoint. The LXX reads καὶ ἐλάλησεν Κορε (kai elalēsen Kore) = "and Korah spoke," apparently reading וַיְדַבֵּר or interpreting freely. The Vulgate has "ecce autem Core." The genealogy traces Korah through Izhar, Kohath, and Levi — making him Moses's first cousin (Moses: son of Amram, son of Kohath). דָּתָן וַאֲבִירָם (Datan va-Aviram) = Reubenite rebels; their camp was adjacent to the Kohathites on the south side of the tabernacle (Num 2:10; 3:29). אוֹן בֶּן פֶּלֶת (On ben Pelet) = mentioned here only and never again in the narrative — the rabbis (b. Sanhedrin 109b-110a) infer that his wife persuaded him to withdraw.

**2** and-they-rose [va-yaqumu] before [lifnei] Moses [Mosheh], and-men [va-anashim] from-the-Sons of [mi-benei] Israel [Yisra'el], fifty [khamishim] and-two-hundred [u-matayim], leaders of [nesi'ei] the-congregation [edah], called-ones of [qeri'ei] the-assembly [mo'ed], men of [anshei] name [shem].

> **Key terms:** וַיָּקֻמוּ לִפְנֵי מֹשֶׁה (va-yaqumu lifnei Mosheh) = "and they rose before Moses" — the qal vav-consecutive of קום (qum, "to rise"), here indicating hostile confrontation. נְשִׂיאֵי עֵדָה (nesi'ei edah) = "leaders of the congregation" — these are not marginal agitators but men of standing, נָשִׂיא (nasi, "prince/chief") being the highest tribal title. קְרִיאֵי מוֹעֵד (qeri'ei mo'ed) = "called ones of the assembly" — the passive qal participle of קרא (qara, "to call"), i.e., those summoned to the council. אַנְשֵׁי שֵׁם (anshei shem) = "men of name," i.e., men of renown. The LXX renders: ἄνδρες ὀνομαστοί ("renowned men"). The number חֲמִשִּׁים וּמָאתַיִם (khamishim u-matayim) = "two hundred and fifty" — a large delegation signaling widespread discontent.

**3** And-they-assembled [va-yiqqahalu] upon [al] Moses [Mosheh] and-upon [ve-al] Aaron [Aharon], and-said [va-yomru] unto-them [alehem], "Much [rav] for-you [lakhem]! For [ki] all [kol] the-congregation [ha-edah], all-of-them [kullam], [are]-holy [qedoshim], and-in-their-midst [u-ve-tokham] [is] YHWH; and-why [u-maddua] do-you-lift-yourselves-up [titnasse'u] over [al] the-assembly of [qehal] YHWH?"

> **Key terms:** וַיִּקָּהֲלוּ עַל־מֹשֶׁה (va-yiqqahalu al Mosheh) = "and they assembled against Moses" — the niphal of קהל (qahal, "to assemble"), with the hostile preposition עַל (al, "upon/against"). רַב־לָכֶם (rav lakhem) = "much for you!" or "enough for you!" — an accusatory exclamation, the same phrase Moses will turn back upon them in v. 7. כִּי כָל־הָעֵדָה כֻּלָּם קְדֹשִׁים (ki kol ha-edah kullam qedoshim) = "for all the congregation, all of them, are holy" — an appeal to the democratization of holiness based on Exodus 19:6 ("a kingdom of priests and a holy nation"). וּבְתוֹכָם יְהוָה (u-ve-tokham YHWH) = "and YHWH is in their midst" — claiming that YHWH's presence among the whole people invalidates the unique mediatorial role of Moses and Aaron. וּמַדּוּעַ תִּתְנַשְּׂאוּ (u-maddua titnasse'u) = "and why do you lift yourselves up?" — the hitpael of נשׂא (nasa, "to lift"), reflexive: "exalt yourselves." **LXX:** ὅτι πᾶσα ἡ συναγωγὴ πάντες ἅγιοι ("because all the congregation, all [are] holy").

## Moses Responds and Proposes the Incense Test (vv. 4-11)

**4** And-heard [va-yishma] Moses [Mosheh], and-fell [va-yippol] upon [al] his-face [panav].

> **Key terms:** וַיִּשְׁמַע מֹשֶׁה וַיִּפֹּל עַל־פָּנָיו (va-yishma Mosheh va-yippol al panav) = "and Moses heard and fell upon his face" — the qal vav-consecutive of שׁמע (shama, "to hear") followed by the qal of נפל (nafal, "to fall"). The prostration is Moses's characteristic response to rebellion (cf. 14:5) — not weakness but intercession and deference to YHWH's judgment.

**5** And-spoke [va-yedabber] unto [el] Korah [Qorakh] and-unto [ve-el] all [kol] his-congregation [adato], saying [le'mor], "Morning [boqer], and-will-make-known [ve-yoda] YHWH [who is] his [et asher lo], and [ve-et] the-holy-one [ha-qadosh], and-he-will-bring-near [ve-hiqriv] unto-him [elav]; and [ve-et] whom [asher] he-will-choose [yivkhar], he-will-bring-near [yaqriv] unto-him [elav].

> **Key terms:** בֹּקֶר וְיֹדַע יְהוָה (boqer ve-yoda YHWH) = "morning, and YHWH will make known" — the noun בֹּקֶר (boqer, "morning") stands as a temporal marker: "[in the] morning." The hifil perfect with vav-consecutive of ידע (yada, "to know"): "he will cause to know / make known." אֶת־אֲשֶׁר־לוֹ (et asher lo) = "the one who is his" — i.e., who belongs to YHWH. וְאֶת־הַקָּדוֹשׁ (ve-et ha-qadosh) = "and the holy one" — the one whom YHWH has sanctified for priestly service. וְהִקְרִיב אֵלָיו (ve-hiqriv elav) = "and he will bring near to himself" — the hifil of קרב (qarav), the technical priestly term for authorized approach to the altar. **LXX:** ἐπέσκεπται καὶ ἔγνω ὁ θεός ("God has visited and known").

**6** This [zot] do [asu]: take [qekhu] for-yourselves [lakhem] fire-pans [makhtot], Korah [Qorakh] and-all [ve-khol] his-congregation [adato],

> **Key terms:** מַחְתּוֹת (makhtot) = "fire-pans / censers" — plural of מַחְתָּה (makhtah), from חתה (khatah, "to snatch up [coals]"). These are the flat, shovel-like pans used to carry burning coals and incense.

**7** and-put [u-tenu] in-them [bahen] fire [esh], and-place [ve-simu] upon-them [aleihen] incense [qetoret] before [lifnei] YHWH tomorrow [makhar]; and-it-shall-be [ve-hayah], the-man [ha-ish] whom [asher] YHWH will-choose [yivkhar] — he [hu] [is] the-holy-one [ha-qadosh]. Much [rav] for-you [lakhem], sons of [benei] Levi [Levi]!"

> **Key terms:** וּתְנוּ בָהֵן אֵשׁ וְשִׂימוּ עֲלֵיהֶן קְטֹרֶת (u-tenu vahen esh ve-simu aleihen qetoret) = "and put fire in them and place incense upon them" — the test: unauthorized incense-offering, the very act for which Nadab and Abihu died (Lev 10:1-2). קְטֹרֶת (qetoret, "incense") from קטר (qatar, "to burn/smoke"). רַב־לָכֶם בְּנֵי לֵוִי (rav lakhem benei Levi) = "much for you, sons of Levi!" — Moses turns their own accusation (v. 3, rav lakhem) back upon them. The ironic reversal is sharp: they said Moses and Aaron had too much; Moses says the Levites themselves are reaching for too much.

**8** And-said [va-yomer] Moses [Mosheh] unto [el] Korah [Qorakh], "Hear [shim'u], please [na], sons of [benei] Levi [Levi]:

> **Key terms:** שִׁמְעוּ־נָא בְּנֵי לֵוִי (shim'u na benei Levi) = "hear, please, sons of Levi" — the qal imperative plural of שׁמע (shama, "to hear") with the entreating particle נָא (na, "please/I pray"). Moses addresses the Levitical faction specifically.

**9** [Is it] too-small [ha-me'at] from-you [mi-kem] that [ki] has-separated [hivdil] the-God of [Elohei] Israel [Yisra'el] you [etkhem] from [me-al] the-congregation of [adat] Israel [Yisra'el], to-bring-near [le-haqriv] you [etkhem] unto-himself [elav], to-serve [la-avod] the-service of [et avodat] the-Tabernacle of [mishkan] YHWH, and-to-stand [ve-la-amod] before [lifnei] the-congregation [ha-edah] to-minister-to-them [le-shartem]?

> **Key terms:** הַמְעַט מִכֶּם (ha-me'at mikkem) = "is it too small a thing from you?" — the interrogative הֲ (ha) + מְעַט (me'at, "small/little"), a rhetorical question. כִּי־הִבְדִּיל (ki hivdil) = "that has separated" — the hifil perfect of בדל (badal, "to separate/distinguish"), the same verb used of God separating light from darkness (Gen 1:4). The Levites have already been "separated" for sacred service — a unique privilege. לַעֲבֹד אֶת־עֲבֹדַת מִשְׁכַּן יְהוָה (la-avod et avodat mishkan YHWH) = "to serve the service of the Tabernacle of YHWH" — cognate accusative construction. וְלַעֲמֹד לִפְנֵי הָעֵדָה לְשָׁרְתָם (ve-la-amod lifnei ha-edah le-shartem) = "and to stand before the congregation to minister to them" — שׁרת (sharat, "to minister/serve") is the technical term for sacred service. **LXX:** μὴ μικρόν ἐστιν τοῦτο ὑμῖν ("is this a small thing to you?").

**10** And-he-brought-near [va-yaqrev] you [otkha] and [ve-et] all [kol] your-brothers [akhekha] the-sons of [benei] Levi [Levi] with-you [ittakh], and-you-seek [u-viqqashtem] also [gam] priesthood [kehunnah]?

> **Key terms:** וַיַּקְרֵב אֹתְךָ וְאֶת־כָּל־אַחֶיךָ בְנֵי־לֵוִי אִתָּךְ (va-yaqrev otkha ve-et kol akhekha benei Levi ittakh) = "and he brought you near, and all your brothers the sons of Levi with you" — the hifil vav-consecutive of קרב (qarav), YHWH as subject: God had already elevated them. וּבִקַּשְׁתֶּם גַּם כְּהֻנָּה (u-viqqashtem gam kehunnah) = "and you seek also priesthood?" — the piel perfect of בקשׁ (biqqesh, "to seek"), with גַּם (gam, "also/even") intensifying the audacity: not content with Levitical service, they grasp for the priesthood itself. כְּהֻנָּה (kehunnah, "priesthood") — the abstract noun from כֹּהֵן (kohen, "priest").

**11** Therefore [lakhen], you [attah] and-all [ve-khol] your-congregation [adatkha], the-ones-assembled [ha-no'adim] against [al] YHWH — and-Aaron [ve-Aharon], what [mah] [is] he [hu] that [ki] you-murmur [tallinu] against-him [alav]?"

> **Key terms:** לָכֵן אַתָּה וְכָל־עֲדָתְךָ הַנֹּעָדִים עַל־יְהוָה (lakhen attah ve-khol adatkha ha-no'adim al YHWH) = "therefore, you and all your congregation, the ones assembled against YHWH" — the niphal participle of יעד (ya'ad, "to appoint/meet"), here with hostile עַל (al): "those banded together against YHWH." Moses redefines their rebellion: it is not against Aaron but against YHWH who appointed Aaron. וְאַהֲרֹן מַה־הוּא כִּי תַלִּינוּ עָלָיו (ve-Aharon mah hu ki tallinu alav) = "and Aaron, what is he that you murmur against him?" — the hifil imperfect of לון/לין (lun/lin, "to murmur/grumble"). Aaron is merely the instrument; the murmuring is ultimately against YHWH.

## Dathan and Abiram's Defiance (vv. 12-15)

**12** And-sent [va-yishlakh] Moses [Mosheh] to-call [liqro] for-Dathan [le-Datan] and-for-Abiram [u-le-Aviram] sons of [benei] Eliab [Eli'av]; and-they-said [va-yomru], "We-will-not [lo] come-up [na'aleh]!

> **Key terms:** וַיִּשְׁלַח מֹשֶׁה לִקְרֹא לְדָתָן וְלַאֲבִירָם (va-yishlakh Mosheh liqro le-Datan u-le-Aviram) = "and Moses sent to call for Dathan and Abiram" — the qal vav-consecutive of שׁלח (shalakh, "to send") followed by the qal infinitive of קרא (qara, "to call"). Moses attempts a separate dialogue with the Reubenite faction. לֹא נַעֲלֶה (lo na'aleh) = "we will not come up" — the qal imperfect of עלה (alah, "to go up"), a flat refusal. The verb "come up" implies Moses's tent or the tabernacle area was on higher ground, or it may be a metaphorical "ascent" to authority. **LXX:** οὐκ ἀναβαίνομεν ("we are not coming up").

**13** [Is it] too-small [ha-me'at] that [ki] you-brought-us-up [he'elitanu] from-a-land [me-erets] flowing [zavat] [with]-milk [khalav] and-honey [u-devash], to-kill-us [la-hamitenu] in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar], that [ki] also [gam] you-lord-over [tishtarer] us [aleinu] — lording [histarer] you-lord [histarer]?

> **Key terms:** הַמְעַט כִּי הֶעֱלִיתָנוּ מֵאֶרֶץ זָבַת חָלָב וּדְבַשׁ (ha-me'at ki he'elitanu me-erets zavat khalav u-devash) = "is it too small that you brought us up from a land flowing with milk and honey?" — a devastating ironic reversal: the phrase "land flowing with milk and honey" normally describes Canaan, but Dathan and Abiram apply it to Egypt, recasting bondage as paradise and liberation as deception. לַהֲמִיתֵנוּ בַּמִּדְבָּר (la-hamitenu ba-midbar) = "to kill us in the wilderness" — the hifil infinitive of מות (mut, "to die"). כִּי־תִשְׂתָּרֵר עָלֵינוּ גַּם־הִשְׂתָּרֵר (ki tishtarer aleinu gam histarer) = "that you also lord-over us — lording you lord?" — the hitpael of שׂרר (sarar, "to rule/be prince"), with the infinitive absolute construction הִשְׂתָּרֵר (histarer) reinforcing the finite verb: "you lord it, you actually lord it!" This is the infinitive absolute doubling convention. **LXX:** ὅτι κατάρχεις ἡμῶν ("that you rule over us").

**14** Also [af] not [lo] unto [el] a-land [erets] flowing [zavat] [with]-milk [khalav] and-honey [u-devash] you-brought-us [havi'otanu], and-[you]-gave [va-titten] to-us [lanu] inheritance of [nakhalat] field [sadeh] and-vineyard [va-karem]. The-eyes of [ha-einei] those [ha-] men [hem] will-you-gouge-out [tinqer]? We-will-not [lo] come-up [na'aleh]!"

> **Key terms:** אַף לֹא אֶל־אֶרֶץ זָבַת חָלָב וּדְבַשׁ הֲבִיאֹתָנוּ (af lo el erets zavat khalav u-devash havi'otanu) = "indeed, not to a land flowing with milk and honey have you brought us" — אַף (af, "indeed/also") intensifies the accusation. They accuse Moses of a double failure: he took them from a good land (Egypt, v. 13) and failed to bring them to another (Canaan). וַתִּתֶּן־לָנוּ נַחֲלַת שָׂדֶה וָכָרֶם (va-titten lanu nakhalat sadeh va-kharem) = "and you gave us inheritance of field and vineyard" — bitterly sarcastic, meaning the opposite. הַעֵינֵי הָאֲנָשִׁים הָהֵם תְּנַקֵּר (ha-einei ha-anashim ha-hem tenaqer) = "will you gouge out the eyes of those men?" — the piel imperfect of נקר (naqar, "to bore/gouge out"). The idiom suggests: "do you think you can blind them to reality?" or "will you deceive them?" The third-person self-reference ("those men" = ourselves) is a rhetorical distancing device. **LXX:** μὴ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἐκείνων ἐξορύξαις ἄν ("would you gouge out the eyes of those men?").

**15** And-was-angry [va-yikhar] to-Moses [le-Mosheh] exceedingly [me'od], and-he-said [va-yomer] unto [el] YHWH, "Do-not [al] turn [tefen] unto [el] their-offering [minkhatam]. Not [lo] one [ekhad] donkey [khamor] of-theirs [mehem] have-I-taken [nasati], and-not [ve-lo] have-I-done-evil [hare'oti] to [et] one [ekhad] of-them [mehem]."

> **Key terms:** וַיִּחַר לְמֹשֶׁה מְאֹד (va-yikhar le-Mosheh me'od) = "and it was exceedingly angry to Moses" — the impersonal qal of חרה (kharah, "to burn/be angry"), with the dative לְ (le-). אַל־תֵּפֶן אֶל־מִנְחָתָם (al tefen el minkhatam) = "do not turn to their offering" — the qal jussive of פנה (panah, "to turn/face") with the negative אַל (al). Moses asks YHWH to reject their incense-offering. לֹא חֲמוֹר אֶחָד מֵהֶם נָשָׂאתִי (lo khamor ekhad mehem nasati) = "not one donkey of theirs have I taken" — the qal perfect of נשׂא (nasa, "to carry/take"). This echoes Samuel's self-defense in 1 Sam 12:3. וְלֹא הֲרֵעֹתִי אֶת־אֶחָד מֵהֶם (ve-lo hare'oti et ekhad mehem) = "and I have not done evil to one of them" — the hifil perfect of רעע (ra'a, "to be evil"). **LXX:** μὴ ἐπιθύμημα ἑνὸς αὐτῶν εἴληφα ("I have not taken a single desired thing of theirs").

## The Incense Test (vv. 16-22)

**16** And-said [va-yomer] Moses [Mosheh] unto [el] Korah [Qorakh], "You [attah] and-all [ve-khol] your-congregation [adatkha], be [heyu] before [lifnei] YHWH — you [attah] and-they [ve-hem] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] — tomorrow [makhar].

> **Key terms:** הֱיוּ לִפְנֵי יְהוָה (heyu lifnei YHWH) = "be before YHWH" — the qal imperative of היה (hayah, "to be"), commanding their presence for the ordeal.

**17** And-take [u-qekhu] each-man [ish] his-fire-pan [makhtato], and-you-shall-put [u-netattem] upon-them [aleihem] incense [qetoret], and-you-shall-bring-near [ve-hiqravtem] before [lifnei] YHWH each-man [ish] his-fire-pan [makhtato] — fifty [khamishim] and-two-hundred [u-matayim] fire-pans [makhtot]; and-you [ve-attah] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon], each-man [ish] his-fire-pan [makhtato]."

> **Key terms:** וּקְחוּ אִישׁ מַחְתָּתוֹ (u-qekhu ish makhtato) = "and take, each man his fire-pan" — the distributive construction with אִישׁ (ish, "man/each"). חֲמִשִּׁים וּמָאתַיִם מַחְתֹּת (khamishim u-matayim makhtot) = "two hundred and fifty fire-pans" — one for each chieftain, plus Korah and Aaron: two hundred fifty-two total participants.

**18** And-took [va-yiqkhu] each-man [ish] his-fire-pan [makhtato], and-they-put [va-yittenu] upon-them [aleihem] fire [esh], and-they-placed [va-yasimu] upon-them [aleihem] incense [qetoret]; and-they-stood [va-ya'amdu] [at] the-entrance of [petakh] the-Tent of [ohel] Meeting [mo'ed], and-Moses [u-Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּעַמְדוּ פֶּתַח אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד (va-ya'amdu petakh ohel mo'ed) = "and they stood at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting" — the qal vav-consecutive of עמד (amad, "to stand"). The entrance of the Tent of Meeting is the place of divine encounter and judgment. וּמֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן (u-Mosheh ve-Aharon) — Moses and Aaron are present as well, standing at the same location.

**19** And-assembled [va-yaqhel] against-them [aleihem] Korah [Qorakh] all [et kol] the-congregation [ha-edah] unto [el] the-entrance of [petakh] the-Tent of [ohel] Meeting [mo'ed]; and-appeared [va-yera] the-glory of [kevod] YHWH unto [el] all [kol] the-congregation [ha-edah].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּקְהֵל עֲלֵיהֶם קֹרַח אֶת־כָּל־הָעֵדָה (va-yaqhel aleihem Qorakh et kol ha-edah) = "and Korah assembled against them all the congregation" — the hifil of קהל (qahal, "to assemble"), causative: Korah gathered the entire congregation against Moses and Aaron. This escalation — from two hundred fifty to "all the congregation" — dramatically raises the stakes. וַיֵּרָא כְבוֹד יְהוָה (va-yera kevod YHWH) = "and the glory of YHWH appeared" — the niphal of ראה (ra'ah, "to see"), the theophanic formula. The appearance of the כָּבוֹד (kavod, "glory") signals imminent divine judgment. **LXX:** καὶ ὤφθη ἡ δόξα κυρίου ("and the glory of the Lord was seen").

**20** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh] and-unto [ve-el] Aaron [Aharon], saying [le'mor]:

**21** "Separate-yourselves [hibbadelu] from-the-midst of [mi-tokh] the-congregation [ha-edah] the-this [ha-zot], and-I-will-consume [va-akhaleh] them [otam] in-a-moment [ke-rega]."

> **Key terms:** הִבָּדְלוּ מִתּוֹךְ הָעֵדָה הַזֹּאת (hibbadelu mi-tokh ha-edah ha-zot) = "separate yourselves from the midst of this congregation" — the niphal imperative of בדל (badal, "to separate"), the same root used in v. 9 for God's separating the Levites. Now God commands separation for judgment. וַאֲכַלֶּה אֹתָם כְּרָגַע (va-akhaleh otam ke-rega) = "and I will consume them in a moment" — the piel cohortative of כלה (kalah, "to complete/consume/destroy") with כְּרָגַע (ke-rega, "as a moment/in an instant"). YHWH threatens to annihilate the entire assembly instantly. **LXX:** καὶ ἐξαναλώσω αὐτοὺς εἰσάπαξ ("and I will utterly destroy them at once").

**22** And-they-fell [va-yippelu] upon [al] their-faces [peneihem], and-they-said [va-yomru], "God [El], God of [Elohei] the-spirits [ha-rukhot] of-all [le-khol] flesh [basar], the-one [ha-ish] man [ekhad] will-sin [yekheta], and-upon [ve-al] all [kol] the-congregation [ha-edah] will-you-be-wrathful [tiqtsof]?"

> **Key terms:** אֵל אֱלֹהֵי הָרוּחֹת לְכָל־בָּשָׂר (El Elohei ha-rukhot le-khol basar) = "God, God of the spirits of all flesh" — a unique divine title appearing only here and in 27:16. It appeals to YHWH as the one who knows the inner spirit (רוּחַ, ruakh) of every living being — and therefore can distinguish the guilty from the innocent. הָאִישׁ אֶחָד יֶחֱטָא (ha-ish ekhad yekheta) = "the one man will sin" — the qal imperfect of חטא (khata, "to sin"). וְעַל כָּל־הָעֵדָה תִּקְצֹף (ve-al kol ha-edah tiqtsof) = "and upon all the congregation will you be wrathful?" — the qal imperfect of קצף (qatsaf, "to be wrathful"). Moses and Aaron intercede: should one man's sin bring judgment on all? **LXX:** ἄνθρωπος εἷς ἥμαρτεν καὶ ἐπὶ πᾶσαν τὴν συναγωγὴν ὀργὴ κυρίου ("one man sinned and upon all the congregation [is] the wrath of the Lord?").

## YHWH's Command to Withdraw (vv. 23-27)

**23** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**24** "Speak [dabber] unto [el] the-congregation [ha-edah], saying [le'mor]: 'Go-up [he'alu] from-around [mi-saviv] the-dwelling of [le-mishkan] Korah [Qorakh], Dathan [Datan], and-Abiram [va-Aviram].'"

> **Key terms:** הֵעָלוּ מִסָּבִיב לְמִשְׁכַּן קֹרַח דָּתָן וַאֲבִירָם (he'alu mi-saviv le-mishkan Qorakh Datan va-Aviram) = "go up from around the dwelling of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram" — the niphal imperative of עלה (alah, "to go up"). מִשְׁכַּן (mishkan) here means "dwelling/habitation" (not the Tabernacle), referring to the tents of the rebels. The LXX reads: ἀναχωρήσατε κύκλῳ ἀπὸ τῆς σκηνῆς Κορε ("withdraw from around the tent of Korah"). Note: the Vaticanus groups Korah with Dathan and Abiram here, while the MT also reads all three names together.

**25** And-rose [va-yaqom] Moses [Mosheh], and-went [va-yelekh] unto [el] Dathan [Datan] and-Abiram [va-Aviram]; and-went [va-yelkhu] after-him [akharav] the-elders of [ziqnei] Israel [Yisra'el].

> **Key terms:** וַיָּקָם מֹשֶׁה וַיֵּלֶךְ אֶל־דָּתָן וַאֲבִירָם (va-yaqom Mosheh va-yelekh el Datan va-Aviram) = "and Moses rose and went to Dathan and Abiram" — since they refused to "come up" (v. 12), Moses goes down to them. וַיֵּלְכוּ אַחֲרָיו זִקְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (va-yelkhu akharav ziqnei Yisra'el) = "and the elders of Israel went after him" — the elders follow as witnesses to the coming judgment.

**26** And-spoke [va-yedabber] unto [el] the-congregation [ha-edah], saying [le'mor], "Turn-aside [suru], please [na], from-upon [me-al] the-tents of [ohalei] the-men [ha-anashim] the-wicked [ha-resha'im] the-these [ha-elleh], and-do-not [ve-al] touch [tigg'u] anything [be-khol] which [asher] is-theirs [lahem], lest [pen] you-be-swept-away [tissafu] in-all [be-khol] their-sins [khattotam]."

> **Key terms:** סוּרוּ נָא מֵעַל אָהֳלֵי הָאֲנָשִׁים הָרְשָׁעִים הָאֵלֶּה (suru na me-al ohalei ha-anashim ha-resha'im ha-elleh) = "turn aside, please, from upon the tents of these wicked men" — the qal imperative of סור (sur, "to turn aside/depart"). Moses publicly names them הָרְשָׁעִים (ha-resha'im, "the wicked"). וְאַל־תִּגְּעוּ בְּכָל־אֲשֶׁר לָהֶם (ve-al tigg'u be-khol asher lahem) = "and do not touch anything which is theirs" — the qal jussive of נגע (naga, "to touch"), recalling the ban (kherem) language. פֶּן־תִּסָּפוּ בְּכָל־חַטֹּאתָם (pen tissafu be-khol khattotam) = "lest you be swept away in all their sins" — the niphal of ספה (safah, "to sweep away/destroy"). Contact with the condemned brings shared destruction. **LXX:** ἀποσχίσθητε ἀπὸ τῶν σκηνῶν τῶν ἀνθρώπων τῶν σκληρῶν τούτων ("separate from the tents of these hard men") — the LXX renders רְשָׁעִים as σκληρῶν ("hard/stubborn") rather than "wicked."

**27** And-they-went-up [va-ye'alu] from-upon [me-al] the-dwelling of [mishkan] Korah [Qorakh], Dathan [Datan], and-Abiram [va-Aviram], from-around [mi-saviv]; and-Dathan [ve-Datan] and-Abiram [va-Aviram] went-out [yats'u], standing [nitsavim] [at] the-entrance of [petakh] their-tents [ohaleihem], and-their-wives [u-nesheihem] and-their-sons [u-veneihem] and-their-little-ones [ve-tappam].

> **Key terms:** וַיֵּעָלוּ מֵעַל מִשְׁכַּן קֹרַח (va-ye'alu me-al mishkan Qorakh) = "and they went up from upon the dwelling of Korah" — the niphal of עלה (alah, "to go up"). The people obey and withdraw. וְדָתָן וַאֲבִירָם יָצְאוּ נִצָּבִים (ve-Datan va-Aviram yats'u nitsavim) = "and Dathan and Abiram went out, standing" — the niphal participle of נצב (natsav, "to stand/take a stand"), conveying defiance. They emerge to face Moses defiantly, with their families beside them. וּנְשֵׁיהֶם וּבְנֵיהֶם וְטַפָּם (u-nesheihem u-veneihem ve-tappam) = "and their wives and their sons and their little ones" — the presence of entire families heightens the horror of what follows.

## The Earth Opens (vv. 28-35)

**28** And-said [va-yomer] Moses [Mosheh], "By-this [be-zot] you-shall-know [ted'un] that [ki] YHWH has-sent-me [shelakhani] to-do [la-asot] all [et kol] the-deeds [ha-ma'asim] the-these [ha-elleh], for [ki] not [lo] from-my-own-heart [mi-libbi]:

> **Key terms:** בְּזֹאת תֵּדְעוּן כִּי יְהוָה שְׁלָחַנִי (be-zot ted'un ki YHWH shelakhani) = "by this you shall know that YHWH has sent me" — the qal imperfect of ידע (yada, "to know") with the 2mp suffix (energic nun). Moses stakes everything on the coming sign. כִּי לֹא מִלִּבִּי (ki lo mi-libbi) = "for not from my own heart" — Moses denies that his authority is self-generated. This directly answers the charge in v. 13 that he "lords it" over them.

**29** If [im] like-the-death of [ke-mot] all [kol] men [ha-adam] these [eileh] will-die [yemutun], and-the-visitation of [u-fequddat] all [kol] men [ha-adam] will-be-visited [yippaqed] upon-them [aleihem], [then] YHWH has-not [lo] sent-me [shelakhani].

> **Key terms:** אִם כְּמוֹת כָּל־הָאָדָם יְמֻתוּן (im ke-mot kol ha-adam yemutun) = "if like the death of all men these will die" — the qal imperfect of מות (mut, "to die") with the conditional אִם (im, "if"). A natural death would disprove Moses's commission. וּפְקֻדַּת כָּל־הָאָדָם יִפָּקֵד עֲלֵיהֶם (u-fequddat kol ha-adam yippaqed aleihem) = "and the visitation of all men is visited upon them" — פְּקֻדָּה (pequddah, "visitation/reckoning"), from פקד (paqad, "to visit/attend to"). The niphal passive: "if ordinary fate befalls them."

**30** But-if [ve-im] a-creation [beriah] YHWH will-create [yivra], and-opens [u-fatsetah] the-ground [ha-adamah] her-mouth [et piha], and-swallows [u-val'ah] them [otam] and [ve-et] all [kol] which [asher] is-theirs [lahem], and-they-go-down [ve-yardu] alive [khayyim] to [the]-pit [she'olah], and-you-will-know [vi-datem] that [ki] have-despised [ni'atsu] the-men [ha-anashim] the-these [ha-elleh] YHWH."

> **Key terms:** וְאִם בְּרִיאָה יִבְרָא יְהוָה (ve-im beriah yivra YHWH) = "but if a creation YHWH will create" — the cognate accusative construction with the qal imperfect of ברא (bara, "to create"). בְּרִיאָה (beriah, "a creation") — something utterly new, unprecedented. This is the same verb used of cosmic creation in Genesis 1:1. וּפָצְתָה הָאֲדָמָה אֶת־פִּיהָ (u-fatsetah ha-adamah et piha) = "and the ground opens her mouth" — the qal perfect with vav-consecutive of פצה (patsah, "to open wide [the mouth]"). The earth is personified as a devouring creature. וּבָלְעָה אֹתָם (u-val'ah otam) = "and swallows them" — the qal of בלע (bala, "to swallow"). וְיָרְדוּ חַיִּים שְׁאֹלָה (ve-yardu khayyim she'olah) = "and they go down alive to Sheol" — שְׁאוֹל (she'ol, "the pit/underworld"), the realm of the dead, with the directional ה (he locale). Going down alive means descending to the underworld without first dying — an unprecedented horror. **LXX:** καὶ καταπίεται αὐτούς, καὶ καταβήσονται ζῶντες εἰς ᾅδου ("and it will swallow them, and they will descend living into Hades").

**31** And-it-was [va-yehi] as-his-finishing [ke-khalloto] to-speak [le-dabber] all [et kol] the-words [ha-devarim] the-these [ha-elleh], and-was-split [va-tibbaqqa] the-ground [ha-adamah] which [asher] under-them [takhtehem].

> **Key terms:** וַיְהִי כְּכַלֹּתוֹ לְדַבֵּר (va-yehi ke-khalloto le-dabber) = "and it was as he finished speaking" — the piel infinitive construct of כלה (kalah, "to finish/complete") with the 3ms suffix. The timing is instantaneous: the moment Moses finishes speaking, the earth acts. וַתִּבָּקַע הָאֲדָמָה אֲשֶׁר תַּחְתֵּיהֶם (va-tibbaqqa ha-adamah asher takhtehem) = "and the ground that was under them was split" — the niphal of בקע (baqa, "to split/cleave"), the same verb used for the splitting of the Red Sea (Exod 14:21). The earth itself bears witness against the rebels.

**32** And-opened [va-tiftakh] the-earth [ha-arets] her-mouth [et piha], and-swallowed [va-tivla] them [otam] and [ve-et] their-houses [batteihem], and [ve-et] all [kol] the-men [ha-adam] who [asher] [belonged]-to-Korah [le-Qorakh] and [ve-et] all [kol] the-property [ha-rekhush].

> **Key terms:** וַתִּפְתַּח הָאָרֶץ אֶת־פִּיהָ (va-tiftakh ha-arets et piha) = "and the earth opened her mouth" — the qal vav-consecutive of פתח (patakh, "to open"), with the earth again personified. The shift from אֲדָמָה (adamah, "ground") to אֶרֶץ (erets, "earth") may indicate a widening of scale. וַתִּבְלַע אֹתָם וְאֶת־בָּתֵּיהֶם (va-tivla otam ve-et batteihem) = "and swallowed them and their houses" — בָּתִּים (battim, "houses") here means households/tents and all within them. וְאֵת כָּל־הָאָדָם אֲשֶׁר לְקֹרַח (ve-et kol ha-adam asher le-Qorakh) = "and all the men who belonged to Korah." וְאֵת כָּל־הָרְכוּשׁ (ve-et kol ha-rekhush) = "and all the property" — רְכוּשׁ (rekhush, "property/possessions"). **LXX note:** The Vaticanus reads the swallowing as encompassing αὐτούς καὶ τοὺς οἴκους αὐτῶν ("them and their houses"). Note: Num 26:11 later states that "the sons of Korah did not die," suggesting a tradition that Korah's children were spared despite the destruction of his faction.

**33** And-went-down [va-yerdu] they [hem] and-all [ve-khol] which [asher] was-theirs [lahem] alive [khayyim] to [the]-pit [she'olah]; and-covered [va-tekhas] upon-them [aleihem] the-earth [ha-arets], and-they-perished [va-yovdu] from-the-midst of [mi-tokh] the-assembly [ha-qahal].

> **Key terms:** וַיֵּרְדוּ הֵם וְכָל־אֲשֶׁר לָהֶם חַיִּים שְׁאֹלָה (va-yerdu hem ve-khol asher lahem khayyim she'olah) = "and they went down, they and all that was theirs, alive to Sheol" — the fulfillment of Moses's sign (v. 30). The repetition of חַיִּים שְׁאֹלָה (khayyim she'olah, "alive to Sheol") emphasizes the horror: not dead bodies descending, but living persons swallowed into the underworld. וַתְּכַס עֲלֵיהֶם הָאָרֶץ (va-tekhas aleihem ha-arets) = "and the earth covered upon them" — the piel of כסה (kasah, "to cover"), the earth closing over them like a grave. וַיֹּאבְדוּ מִתּוֹךְ הַקָּהָל (va-yovdu mi-tokh ha-qahal) = "and they perished from the midst of the assembly" — the qal of אבד (avad, "to perish"). They are erased from the congregation.

**34** And-all [ve-khol] Israel [Yisra'el] who [asher] [were]-around-them [sevivotehem] fled [nasu] at-their-voice [le-qolam], for [ki] they-said [amru], "Lest [pen] the-earth [ha-arets] swallow-us [tivlaenu]!"

> **Key terms:** וְכָל־יִשְׂרָאֵל אֲשֶׁר סְבִיבֹתֵיהֶם נָסוּ לְקֹלָם (ve-khol Yisra'el asher sevivotehem nasu le-qolam) = "and all Israel who were around them fled at their voice/sound" — the qal perfect of נוס (nus, "to flee"). לְקֹלָם (le-qolam) = "at their voice/cry" — the screaming of those being swallowed alive. פֶּן־תִּבְלָעֵנוּ הָאָרֶץ (pen tivlaenu ha-arets) = "lest the earth swallow us" — the collective terror that the judgment might extend to bystanders.

**35** And-a-fire [ve-esh] went-out [yats'ah] from-with [me-et] YHWH, and-consumed [va-tokhal] the [et] fifty [khamishim] and-two-hundred [u-matayim] men [ish], the-ones-offering [maqrivei] the-incense [ha-qetoret].

> **Key terms:** וְאֵשׁ יָצְאָה מֵאֵת יְהוָה (ve-esh yats'ah me-et YHWH) = "and a fire went out from with YHWH" — the qal perfect of יצא (yatsa, "to go out"). Divine fire, the same instrument that consumed Nadab and Abihu (Lev 10:2). This is the second mode of judgment: the earth swallowed the household rebels (vv. 31-34), and fire consumed the incense-offerers. וַתֹּאכַל אֵת הַחֲמִשִּׁים וּמָאתַיִם אִישׁ מַקְרִיבֵי הַקְּטֹרֶת (va-tokhal et ha-khamishim u-matayim ish maqrivei ha-qetoret) = "and consumed the two hundred and fifty men, the ones offering the incense" — the hifil participle of קרב (qarav, "to bring near"), used substantively: "those who brought near the incense." **LXX:** καὶ πῦρ ἐξῆλθεν παρὰ κυρίου καὶ κατέφαγεν τοὺς πεντήκοντα καὶ διακοσίους ἄνδρας ("and fire went out from [the] Lord and consumed the two hundred and fifty men").

## The Fire-Pans as a Sign (vv. 36-40)

**36** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**37** "Say [emor] unto [el] Eleazar [El'azar] son of [ben] Aaron [Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen], and-let-him-pick-up [ve-yaremm] the [et] fire-pans [ha-makhtot] from-between [mi-bein] the-burning [ha-serephah], and-the-fire [ve-et ha-esh] scatter [zereh] further-away [hal'ah], for [ki] they-have-become-holy [qadesu].

> **Key terms:** וְיָרֵם אֶת־הַמַּחְתֹּת מִבֵּין הַשְּׂרֵפָה (ve-yaremm et ha-makhtot mi-bein ha-serephah) = "and let him pick up the fire-pans from between the burning" — the hifil jussive of רום (rum, "to be high/lift up"). שְׂרֵפָה (serephah, "burning"), from שׂרף (saraf, "to burn"). וְאֶת־הָאֵשׁ זְרֵה הָלְאָה (ve-et ha-esh zereh hal'ah) = "and the fire scatter further away" — the qal imperative of זרה (zarah, "to scatter/winnow"). The coals are to be scattered because they are not holy; the pans themselves are. כִּי קָדֵשׁוּ (ki qadesu) = "for they have become holy" — the qal perfect of קדשׁ (qadash, "to be holy"). The fire-pans were sanctified by their presentation before YHWH, even though the offerers were judged. Eleazar (not Aaron) handles the pans, perhaps to avoid contact with the dead for the high priest.

**38** The [et] fire-pans of [makhtot] the-sinners [ha-khatta'im] the-these [ha-elleh] against-their-souls [be-nafshotehem], and-they-shall-make [ve-asu] them [otam] hammered-plates [riqqe'ei] pakhim [thin-sheets], a-covering [tsippuy] for-the-altar [la-mizbe'akh] — for [ki] they-brought-them-near [hiqrivum] before [lifnei] YHWH, and-they-became-holy [va-yiqdashu]; and-they-shall-be [ve-yiheyu] as-a-sign [le-ot] for-the-Sons of [li-vnei] Israel [Yisra'el]."

> **Key terms:** אֵת מַחְתּוֹת הַחַטָּאִים הָאֵלֶּה בְּנַפְשֹׁתָם (et makhtot ha-khatta'im ha-elleh be-nafshotehem) = "the fire-pans of these sinners against their own souls" — בְּנַפְשֹׁתָם (be-nafshotehem) = "against/with their souls/lives," i.e., at the cost of their lives. חַטָּאִים (khatta'im, "sinners") from חטא (khata, "to sin"). רִקְּעֵי פַחִים (riqqe'ei pakhim) = "hammered thin-sheets" — the piel passive participle of רקע (raqa, "to beat/spread out") + פַּח (pakh, "thin plate"). The censers are to be hammered into plating for the altar. צִפּוּי לַמִּזְבֵּחַ (tsippuy la-mizbe'akh) = "a covering for the altar" — צִפּוּי (tsippuy, "overlay/plating"), from צפה (tsafah, "to overlay"). וְיִהְיוּ לְאוֹת לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (ve-yiheyu le-ot li-vnei Yisra'el) = "and they shall be as a sign for the Sons of Israel" — a permanent memorial warning against unauthorized priestly approach.

**39** And-took [va-yiqqakh] Eleazar [El'azar] the-priest [ha-kohen] the [et] fire-pans of [makhtot] the-bronze [ha-nekhoshet] which [asher] brought-near [hiqrivu] the-ones-who-were-burned [ha-seruphim], and-they-hammered-them [va-yeraqqe'um] a-covering [tsippuy] for-the-altar [la-mizbe'akh],

> **Key terms:** מַחְתּוֹת הַנְּחֹשֶׁת (makhtot ha-nekhoshet) = "the fire-pans of bronze" — confirming the material. הַשְּׂרֻפִים (ha-seruphim) = "the ones who were burned" — the qal passive participle of שׂרף (saraf, "to burn"), describing the two hundred fifty men consumed by divine fire. וַיְרַקְּעוּם (va-yeraqqe'um) = "and they hammered them" — the piel of רקע (raqa, "to beat/hammer out").

**40** a-memorial [zikkaron] for-the-Sons of [li-vnei] Israel [Yisra'el], so-that [le-ma'an] not [lo] shall-come-near [yiqrav] a-man [ish] strange [zar] who [asher] [is] not [lo] from-the-seed of [mi-zera] Aaron [Aharon] he [hu], to-burn [le-haqtir] incense [qetoret] before [lifnei] YHWH, and-he-will-not [ve-lo] be [yihyeh] like-Korah [ke-Qorakh] and-like-his-congregation [ve-kha-adato], as-which [ka-asher] spoke [dibber] YHWH by-the-hand of [be-yad] Moses [Mosheh] to-him [lo].

> **Key terms:** זִכָּרוֹן לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (zikkaron li-vnei Yisra'el) = "a memorial for the Sons of Israel" — from זכר (zakhar, "to remember"). The bronze plating on the altar serves as a perpetual warning. לְמַעַן לֹא־יִקְרַב אִישׁ זָר (le-ma'an lo yiqrav ish zar) = "so that no strange man shall come near" — זָר (zar, "stranger/outsider"), here meaning anyone not of Aaronic descent. אֲשֶׁר לֹא מִזֶּרַע אַהֲרֹן (asher lo mi-zera Aharon) = "who is not from the seed of Aaron." לְהַקְטִיר קְטֹרֶת (le-haqtir qetoret) = "to burn incense" — the hifil infinitive of קטר (qatar, "to burn/make smoke"), the technical term for incense-burning. The warning: no one outside Aaron's line may offer incense, or they will share Korah's fate. **LXX:** ὅπως ἂν μὴ προσέλθῃ μηθεὶς ἀλλογενής ("so that no one of another race may approach").

## The Congregation Murmurs — The Plague (vv. 41-50)

**41** And-murmured [va-yillonu] all [kol] the-congregation of [adat] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] on-the-morrow [mi-makhorat] against [al] Moses [Mosheh] and-against [ve-al] Aaron [Aharon], saying [le'mor], "You [attem] have-killed [hamittem] the [et] people of [am] YHWH."

> **Key terms:** וַיִּלֹּנוּ כָּל־עֲדַת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל מִמָּחֳרָת (va-yillonu kol adat benei Yisra'el mi-makhorat) = "and all the congregation of the Sons of Israel murmured on the morrow" — the niphal vav-consecutive of לון (lun, "to murmur/grumble"). Astonishingly, the very day after the catastrophic judgment, the people blame Moses and Aaron rather than the rebels. מִמָּחֳרָת (mi-makhorat, "on the morrow") — the timing underscores the people's incorrigibility. אַתֶּם הֲמִתֶּם אֶת־עַם יְהוָה (attem hamittem et am YHWH) = "you have killed the people of YHWH" — the hifil perfect of מות (mut, "to die"). They call the rebel dead "the people of YHWH," directly contradicting YHWH's own judgment. **LXX:** ὑμεῖς ἀπεκτάγκατε τὸν λαὸν κυρίου ("you have killed the people of the Lord").

**42** And-it-was [va-yehi] when-assembled [be-hiqqahel] the-congregation [ha-edah] against [al] Moses [Mosheh] and-against [ve-al] Aaron [Aharon], and-they-turned [va-yifnu] unto [el] the-Tent of [ohel] Meeting [mo'ed], and-behold [ve-hinneh], covered-it [kissahu] the-cloud [he-anan], and-appeared [va-yera] the-glory of [kevod] YHWH.

> **Key terms:** בְּהִקָּהֵל הָעֵדָה (be-hiqqahel ha-edah) = "when the congregation assembled" — the niphal infinitive construct of קהל (qahal, "to assemble"). וַיִּפְנוּ אֶל־אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד (va-yifnu el ohel mo'ed) = "and they turned to the Tent of Meeting" — the qal of פנה (panah, "to turn"). וְהִנֵּה כִסָּהוּ הֶעָנָן (ve-hinneh kissahu he-anan) = "and behold, the cloud covered it" — the piel perfect of כסה (kasah, "to cover"), with the cloud descending upon the Tent. וַיֵּרָא כְּבוֹד יְהוָה (va-yera kevod YHWH) = "and the glory of YHWH appeared" — the same theophanic formula as v. 19, now signaling a second round of judgment.

**43** And-came [va-yavo] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] unto [el] the-face of [penei] the-Tent of [ohel] Meeting [mo'ed].

**44** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**45** "Get-away [herommu] from-the-midst of [mi-tokh] the-congregation [ha-edah] the-this [ha-zot], and-I-will-consume [va-akhaleh] them [otam] in-a-moment [ke-rega]." And-they-fell [va-yippelu] upon [al] their-faces [peneihem].

> **Key terms:** הֵרֹמּוּ מִתּוֹךְ הָעֵדָה הַזֹּאת (herommu mi-tokh ha-edah ha-zot) = "get away from the midst of this congregation" — the niphal imperative of רום (rum, "to be high/rise"), here meaning "remove yourselves / get up away." This echoes the command in v. 21 (הִבָּדְלוּ, "separate yourselves"), but with a different verb. וַאֲכַלֶּה אֹתָם כְּרָגַע (va-akhaleh otam ke-rega) = "and I will consume them in a moment" — identical to v. 21. YHWH again threatens total annihilation. Moses and Aaron again fall on their faces in intercession.

**46** And-said [va-yomer] Moses [Mosheh] unto [el] Aaron [Aharon], "Take [qakh] the [et] fire-pan [ha-makhtah], and-put [ve-ten] upon-it [aleha] fire [esh] from-upon [me-al] the-altar [ha-mizbe'akh], and-place [ve-sim] incense [qetoret], and-go [ve-holekh] quickly [meherah] unto [el] the-congregation [ha-edah], and-make-atonement [ve-khapper] upon-them [aleihem]; for [ki] has-gone-out [yatsa] the-wrath [ha-qetsef] from-before [mi-lifnei] YHWH — has-begun [hekhel] the-plague [ha-negef]!"

> **Key terms:** קַח אֶת־הַמַּחְתָּה (qakh et ha-makhtah) = "take the fire-pan" — the qal imperative of לקח (laqakh, "to take"). The very instrument that was used in unauthorized offering now becomes the instrument of atonement — but only in Aaron's authorized hands. וְשִׂים קְטֹרֶת (ve-sim qetoret) = "and place incense" — the same act (incense-burning) that killed the two hundred fifty now saves the people. The difference is authorization. וְכַפֵּר עֲלֵיהֶם (ve-khapper aleihem) = "and make atonement upon them" — the piel imperative of כפר (kafar/kipper, "to cover/atone"). כִּי יָצָא הַקֶּצֶף מִלִּפְנֵי יְהוָה (ki yatsa ha-qetsef mi-lifnei YHWH) = "for the wrath has gone out from before YHWH" — קֶצֶף (qetsef, "wrath/anger"), from קצף (qatsaf, "to be angry"). הֵחֵל הַנָּגֶף (hekhel ha-negef) = "the plague has begun" — the hifil perfect of חלל (khalal, "to begin") + נֶגֶף (negef, "plague/smiting"). **LXX:** ὅτι ἐξῆλθεν ὀργὴ ἀπὸ προσώπου κυρίου, ἦρκται θραύειν τὸν λαόν ("for wrath has gone out from the face of the Lord; it has begun to destroy the people").

**47** And-took [va-yiqqakh] Aaron [Aharon] as-which [ka-asher] spoke [dibber] Moses [Mosheh], and-ran [va-yarats] unto [el] the-midst of [tokh] the-assembly [ha-qahal], and-behold [ve-hinneh], had-begun [hekhel] the-plague [ha-negef] among-the-people [ba-am]; and-he-put [va-yitten] the [et] incense [ha-qetoret], and-made-atonement [va-yekhapper] upon [al] the-people [ha-am].

> **Key terms:** וַיָּרָץ אֶל־תּוֹךְ הַקָּהָל (va-yarats el tokh ha-qahal) = "and he ran into the midst of the assembly" — the qal vav-consecutive of רוץ (ruts, "to run"). Aaron runs toward death, not away from it — a dramatic image of priestly intercession. While the people flee from the plague, the high priest runs into it. וְהִנֵּה הֵחֵל הַנֶּגֶף בָּעָם (ve-hinneh hekhel ha-negef ba-am) = "and behold, the plague had begun among the people." וַיִּתֵּן אֶת־הַקְּטֹרֶת (va-yitten et ha-qetoret) = "and he put the incense" — on the fire-pan. וַיְכַפֵּר עַל־הָעָם (va-yekhapper al ha-am) = "and he made atonement upon the people" — the piel vav-consecutive of כפר (kipper, "to atone"). The incense-atonement is effective where the unauthorized incense was lethal.

**48** And-stood [va-ya'amod] between [bein] the-dead [ha-metim] and-between [u-vein] the-living [ha-khayyim], and-was-restrained [va-te'atsar] the-plague [ha-maggephah].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּעֲמֹד בֵּין הַמֵּתִים וּבֵין הַחַיִּים (va-ya'amod bein ha-metim u-vein ha-khayyim) = "and he stood between the dead and the living" — the qal vav-consecutive of עמד (amad, "to stand"). This is one of the most iconic images in the Torah: Aaron standing as a living boundary between death and life, the priest as intercessor holding back the advance of divine judgment with a censer of incense. וַתֵּעָצַר הַמַּגֵּפָה (va-te'atsar ha-maggephah) = "and the plague was restrained" — the niphal of עצר (atsar, "to restrain/hold back"). מַגֵּפָה (maggephah, "plague/smiting") from נגף (nagaf, "to strike/smite"). **LXX:** καὶ ἔστη ἀνὰ μέσον τῶν τεθνηκότων καὶ τῶν ζώντων, καὶ ἐκόπασεν ἡ θραῦσις ("and he stood in the midst of the dead and the living, and the destruction ceased").

**49** And-were [va-yiheyu] the-dead [ha-metim] in-the-plague [ba-maggephah] four-[arba'ah]-teen [asar] thousand [elef] and-seven [u-sheva] hundred [me'ot], apart-from [milvad] the-dead [ha-metim] on [al] the-matter of [devar] Korah [Qorakh].

> **Key terms:** וַיִּהְיוּ הַמֵּתִים בַּמַּגֵּפָה אַרְבָּעָה עָשָׂר אֶלֶף וּשְׁבַע מֵאוֹת (va-yiheyu ha-metim ba-maggephah arba'ah asar elef u-sheva me'ot) = "and the dead in the plague were fourteen thousand and seven hundred" — fourteen thousand seven hundred people, an enormous toll. מִלְּבַד הַמֵּתִים עַל דְּבַר קֹרַח (milvad ha-metim al devar Qorakh) = "apart from the dead on the matter of Korah" — the plague casualties are in addition to those swallowed by the earth and burned by fire. The total death toll from the combined rebellion is fourteen thousand seven hundred plus two hundred fifty plus the households of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.

**50** And-returned [va-yashov] Aaron [Aharon] unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], unto [el] the-entrance of [petakh] the-Tent of [ohel] Meeting [mo'ed]; and-the-plague [ve-ha-maggephah] was-restrained [ne'etsarah].

> **Key terms:** וַיָּשָׁב אַהֲרֹן אֶל מֹשֶׁה אֶל פֶּתַח אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד (va-yashov Aharon el Mosheh el petakh ohel mo'ed) = "and Aaron returned to Moses, to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting" — the qal vav-consecutive of שׁוב (shuv, "to return"). Aaron's mission of atonement is complete. וְהַמַּגֵּפָה נֶעֱצָרָה (ve-ha-maggephah ne'etsarah) = "and the plague was restrained" — the niphal perfect of עצר (atsar, "to restrain"), the concluding statement. The chapter that began with rebellion ends with the plague stopped and the priesthood vindicated. **LXX:** καὶ ἡ θραῦσις ἐκεκόπακεν ("and the destruction had ceased").

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **The objectless verb in v. 1 (וַיִּקַּח / va-yiqqakh):** The Leningrad Codex reads וַיִּקַּח קֹרַח without an explicit direct object. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads καὶ ἐλάλησεν Κορε ("and Korah spoke"), suggesting the translators either had a different Vorlage or paraphrased to resolve the difficulty. The Samaritan Pentateuch and Targum Onqelos also read "took" but supply various implied objects. I have preserved the MT's abruptness, which may be intentionally disruptive — the grammar itself enacts the chaos of the rebellion.

2. **Korah, Dathan, and Abiram — Composite or Unified Narrative:** The MT presents a single narrative, but the distinct rebel groups (Korah the Levite challenging Aaron's priesthood; Dathan and Abiram the Reubenites challenging Moses's civil authority) have led scholars to posit originally separate accounts woven together. The Vaticanus text follows the same basic narrative sequence as the MT. I have translated the unified Hebrew text as it stands in the Leningrad Codex.

3. **On ben Peleth (v. 1):** Named only in v. 1 and never again mentioned in the narrative. The rabbis explained his disappearance through a tradition that his wife dissuaded him (b. Sanhedrin 109b-110a). The LXX reads Αυν υἱὸς Φαλεθ. His absence from the remainder of the chapter is preserved without harmonization.

4. **"Sons of Korah did not die" (cf. Num 26:11):** The present chapter makes no mention of survivors from Korah's household, and v. 32 states the earth swallowed "all the men who belonged to Korah." The later notice in 26:11 that Korah's sons survived represents either a different tradition or implies they separated from their father before the judgment. The Psalms attributed to the "Sons of Korah" (Pss 42, 44-49, 84-85, 87-88) testify to this surviving line.

5. **The infinitive absolute in v. 13 (הִשְׂתָּרֵר / histarer):** The hitpael infinitive absolute reinforcing the finite verb כִּי־תִשְׂתָּרֵר ... גַּם הִשְׂתָּרֵר — rendered with the doubling convention: "you lord-over ... lording you lord." This preserves the emphatic Hebrew construction.

6. **Verse numbering (vv. 36-40 / 17:1-5):** In the Hebrew Bible (BHS), what English versions number as 16:36-50 is numbered 17:1-15. I have followed the English versification (16:36-50) for consistency with the EMET translation convention, while noting that the Hebrew chapter division places the fire-pan memorial and plague narrative at the beginning of chapter 17.

7. **"God of the spirits of all flesh" (v. 22):** The unique title אֵל אֱלֹהֵי הָרוּחֹת לְכָל־בָּשָׂר (El Elohei ha-rukhot le-khol basar) appears only here and in Num 27:16. The LXX renders θεὸς τῶν πνευμάτων καὶ πάσης σαρκός ("God of the spirits and of all flesh"). This title appeals to YHWH's intimate knowledge of every individual's inner spirit — the theological foundation for distinguishing the guilty from the innocent.

8. **The two words for "plague" (vv. 46-50):** The Hebrew uses both נֶגֶף (negef, vv. 46-47) and מַגֵּפָה (maggephah, vv. 48-50), both from the root נגף (nagaf, "to strike/smite"). I have rendered both as "plague" for consistency, though negef emphasizes the divine striking and maggephah the resulting condition.

9. **LXX chapter numbering:** In the Vaticanus, Numbers 16 corresponds to the end of LXX chapter XIX and the beginning of chapter XX. The page images (pa_0124 and pa_0125) show the transition with the chapter marking visible. The LXX text is generally faithful to the MT in this chapter, with the notable exception of the opening verb (v. 1) and the rendering of רְשָׁעִים as σκληρῶν ("hard/stubborn") rather than "wicked" in v. 26.

10. **The ironic reversal of רַב לָכֶם (rav lakhem):** In v. 3, the rebels say רַב לָכֶם ("too much for you") to Moses and Aaron. In v. 7, Moses turns the identical phrase back upon Korah: רַב לָכֶם בְּנֵי לֵוִי ("too much for you, sons of Levi"). This rhetorical reversal is preserved exactly in the translation.
